Monsoon ke badal Kothivaram gaon ke upar neeche tak dabaye hue the, aasman ko ek dard bhari si neeli-kali dhabba bana diya tha. Hawa mein barish se bhigi mitti ki gehri, dhaatu jaisi khushboo thi, aur khet ke kinare pe lage bargad ke pedon se kaun ke dheeme, be-tarteeb kaav aawazein aa rahi thi — woh kaun jo migrate karne se inkaar kar chuke the.

Kothivaram waisi jagah nahi thi jahan bahar ke log der tak thaharte. Sadak crack ho chuki thi, tooti hui, jaise koi warning de rahi ho, aur log “jo chhod diya jaaye woh chhod do” ke baare mein dheemi awaazon mein baat karte the. Lekin apni chuppi ke bawajood, gaon mein ek cheez thi jo har mehmaan ki nazar ko pakad leti: woh kuan.

Woh gaon ke chowk ke dil mein khada tha, yaadon se bhi purana, uske patthar ke munh par kaii saalon se ug aayi kaliyon ki pattiyan thi, aur uska base waqt ke saath ghisa hua. Bachchon ko uske paas khelne ki manaahat thi — na ki isliye ki woh gir jayenge, balki isliye ki gaon walon ke kyahte the: “Yeh kuan jo chahe le leta hai.”

Radha ne yeh kahaaniyaan sun kar palayi thi.

Ab 26 saal ki, woh lagbhag das saal baad Kothivaram laut aayi thi, shahar ki zindagi qarzon aur dhokhon mein doob chuki thi. Pitaji ki achank maut uske lautne ka bahana ban gayi, lekin sach to yeh tha ki uske andar apne liye zyada gham tha unke liye nahi.

Jab woh bus se utri, to sabse pehle uski nazar ussi kuan par padi. Uske aas-paas suraksha ke liye phoolon ki maala nahi thi, na hi uske kinare par jhanda bandha hua tha — bas ek khula hua munh, intezaar kar raha tha.

“Zyada der tak mat ghoor,” peeche se ek awaaz aayi.

Radha ne mud kar dekha. Sundaram tha, gaon ka pandit. Uske baal safed ho chuke the, lekin uska kamar ab bhi waisa hi seedha tha jaisa bachpan mein tha. Uski nazar Radha par nahi, balki kuan par thi.

“Ab bhi wahi purani kahaaniyaan, kaka?” Radha ne muskurane ki koshish ki, lekin uski awaaz phatti.

Sundaram ki aankhein akhirkaar uski taraf mudin. “Kahaaniyaan? Jis din tum chali gayi thi, usne Meenakshi ka beta le liya. Woh paani lene gaya tha. Unhe kabhi nahi mila. Na hi rope wapas aaya.”

Radha ne majboori mein hansne ki koshish ki. “Shayad woh bhaag gaya. Yeh gaon logon ko suffocate kar dete hain.”

Lekin Sundaram nahi hasa. Uski khamoshi ne us par namki hawa se zyada dabav daala.

—

Uske pitaji ka ghar bilkul waisa hi tha jaisa woh yaad rakhti thi — lakdi ke beams jhuke hue, hawa mein geeli daal aur bujhi agarbatti ki khushboo. Usne threshold ko chhua, ek adhoori si dua phuslaayi, aur andar kadam rakha.

Deewar par uski maa ka portrait lata tha — aankhein sakht, honth seenche hue. Maa jawaan umar mein bukhaar se mar gayi thi, lekin Radha ko yaad tha ki woh har shaam ek diya jalati thi, kuan ki taraf dekh kar, aise shabd phuslati thi jo ghar ke kisi aur ko sunne ka haq nahi tha.

Radha ne apna suitcase khol kar rakh diya, aur drawer mein apni purani diary nikali — neem ke daag se kharab ho chuki. Usne be-dimaagi se usmein pages palte hue dekha, jab tak ek page par ruk gayi — bachchon jaisi likhai thi:

“Kuan se paani mat piyo. Raat ko andar mat dekho. Agar yeh tumhara naam pukare to jawab mat dena.”

Usne brows furrow kiye. Use yaad nahi tha ki usne yeh likha tha.

—

Us raat, jab monsoon ne chhat par tez tez vaar kiye aur bijli aasman par rengti hui chali gayi, Radha charpai par jaagti rahi. Gaon bahut chup tha, unnaturally chup. Aam taur par bulbulon ka chorus bhi nahi tha.

Aadhi raat ke baad, ek awaaz ne use jagaya.

Thup. Thup. Thup.

Jaise paani tapak raha ho.

Radha uthi aur awaaz ke peeche chali, pairon ke talein thandi pathar ki feel. Jaise hi woh chowk ki taraf wale window ke paas pahunchi, awaaz tez ho gayi.

Usne curtain hata kar dekha.

Bijli ki chamak mein kuan chamak raha tha. Lekin jis cheez ne uske seenay ko tight kar diya, woh yeh nahi thi.

Wahan, uske kinare, ek figure khadi thi. Ek bachcha. Chhota, be-harakat, sar tilta hua jaise kuch sun raha ho.

Uski saans ruk gayi. Usne Sundaram ke shabd yaad kiye — Meenakshi ka ladka, jo das saal pehle nigal liya gaya tha.

Apne aap ko rokne se pehle, usne phuslaaya, “Wahan kaun hai?”

Bachcha ne apna sar ghumaaya. Toofan ke bawajood, Radha ne use saaf dekha: chamaDi faqa, honth phatte hue, aankhein bahut badi, paani se bhari hui.

Usne dheere se apna haath uthaaya, aur Radha ki window ki taraf point kiya.

Agli bijli ki chamak ne sab kuch andhera kar diya.

—

Subah ek unnatural calm layi. Gaon walay apne kaam mein busy the, lekin jab woh guzarti to unki nazarin jhuka kar chalti thi. Radha khud ko phir se chowk ki taraf khincha gaya. Kuan waisa hi khada tha, barish se geeli kaliyon wale patthar.

Usne apne pet mein ghadgadhahat ke bawajood uski taraf kadam badhaya.

Rope aur bucket abhi bhi wahan latke the. Usne rope ko halka sa kheencha — geela, frayed, brittle. Bina soche samjhe, woh jhuk kar andar jhaanki.

Neeche paani unnatural tarah se chamak raha tha, jaise saans le raha ho.

Aur phir, dheemi lekin saaf, ek awaaz aayi.

Apni hi.

“Radha.”

—

Chapter Two – The Whispering Rope

Radha ne kuan se peeche hat kar apne seenay ko pakda. Apne naam ki goonj ab bhi uske kaanon mein lehrati thi. Use dismiss karna chahta tha — thakan, ya kalpana ka khel — lekin uske andar kuch use rok raha tha.

Kal gaon walon ne uski wapasi par patli muskuraahat, polite shabd, aur jaldi se hat jaane wali nazaron se dekha tha. Ab, chowk ke kinare khadi, use wahi invisible wall of silence mehsoos ho rahi thi.

“Phir se dekh rahi ho?”

Awaaz peeche se aayi. Radha ne mud kar dekha — Sita mami, uske pitaji ki widow cousin, apne hip par supari ki tokri sambhaale. Budhi aurat ki chamaDi jhuri hui thi, lekin uski aankhein tez thi.

“Agar tum itna jhukti raho gi to tum gir jaaogi,” Sita ne kaha.

Radha ne majboori se hansne ki koshish ki. “Yahan sab log kyun boltay hain jaise kuan zinda hai?”

Sita ka chehra sakht ho gaya. “Kyunki woh hai.”

—

Us din baad mein, Radha apne pitaji ke ghar ki dhool bhari verandah saaf kar rahi thi, ordinary kaamon mein kho jaane ki koshish kar rahi thi. Lekin uski nazar baar-baar chowk ki taraf mudti rahi. Kuan sab kuch dominate kar raha tha. Dukaan, mandir, yahan tak ki paas ka neem ka ped bhi jaise uske gird position kiye gaye hon, jaise sitaron ka black sun ke gird.

Shaam ko, Sundaram aaya. Usne phalon ki ek tokri laayi aur wooden stool par rakh di. Uski maujoodgi ne kamre ko bechaini se bhar diya.

“Tumhare pitaji marne se pehle is ghar ko bechna chahte the,” pandit ne kaha. “Lekin koi khareedne wala nahi mila. Sab jaante hain ki tumhari bloodline… tied hai.”

Radha ne brows furrow kiye. “Tied kaise?”

Sundaram ki aankhein shutter wale window ki taraf darti hui thi jo chowk ki taraf tha. “Kuan se. Tumhare daada ji last the jo usmein offerings daalte the.”

“Kaunsi offerings?”

“Jis bare mein ab koi baat nahi karta.”

Woh aur paas aaya. Uski saans mein tulsi aur raakh ki halki si khushboo thi. “Batao, kal raat tumne suna tha na?”

Radha freeze ho gayi.

Sundaram ne uski khamoshi ko jawab samajh liya. Uska haath thoda sa kaampa jab usne apne satchel se ek kali hui rope nikali, kinare se frayed. Usne use dono ke beech stool par rakh diya.

“Yeh teen saal pehle kuan se nikali gayi thi,” usne kaha. “Jab upar aayi to geeli thi. Aur usne us aadmi ka naam phuslaaya jo use pakde hua tha. Usne agle din khudko phaansa laga liya.”

Radha ko apna pet ulta hone laga. Usne rope ko dhakka de diya, lekin rough fibers uske haath se chipakte rah gaye even after she let go.

“Isko chhod do, Radha,” Sundaram ne ab urgent awaaz mein kaha. “Jhuk kar mat dekho. Suno mat.”

Pehle ki woh use aur sawaal kar paati, woh utha, ek dua phuslaaya, aur chala gaya.

—

Us raat, toofan wapas aaya. Radha oil lamp ke paas baithi, diary uski god mein khuli hui. Usne phir se woh page padha:

“Kuan se paani mat piyo. Raat ko andar mat dekho. Agar yeh tumhara naam pukare to jawab mat dena.”

Shabd ab ek bachche ki warning jaisa nahi, balki generations deep pass down ki gayi command jaisa lag raha tha.

Lamp flicker hua. Usne phir woh awaaz suni.

Thup. Thup. Thup.

Paani tapak raha tha. Lekin iss baar, yeh bahar se nahi, ghar ke andar se aa rahi thi.

Radha uthi, dil tez dhadak raha tha. Usne awaaz ke peeche narrow hall se guzar kar pitaji ke purane prayer room tak kiya. Darwaza cheekh kar khula.

Andar ki hawa mein dampness ki tez, dhaatu jaisi boo thi. Corner mein, ek brass lota ulta pada tha, uske gird paani faila hua tha. Lekin awaaz lota se nahi aa rahi thi.

Woh deewar se aa rahi thi.

Radha ne apna kaan crack wali plaster par press kiya.

Deewar geeli thi. Aur uske andar se, dheemi lekin undeniable, ek phusphusahat aayi.

Apni hi awaaz.

“Radha.”

—

Woh peeche latki, lamp ko giraa diya. Flame hiss karke bujh gayi, ghar ko andhera kar diya.

Aur phir, chowk se ek aur awaaz aayi.

Ek rope ki cheekh. Slowly kheenchti ja rahi thi, jaise koi anjaan cheez kuan se paani kheench rahi ho.

Radha ki saans tez ho gayi. Usne khud ko window tak majboor kiya.

Toofan mein kuan khada tha. Rope khud move kar rahi thi, inch-by-inch upar uth rahi thi. Koi pakde hua nahi tha. Bucket surface par aaya, kaale paani se bhara hua.

Aur us paani mein tair rahi thi ek cheez.

Ek diary. Uski jaisi hi.

Door se bhi, usne dekha ki geeli pages khul rahi thi. Storm ki hawa ne shabd uske kaanon tak pahuncha diye.

“Agar yeh tumhara naam pukare to jawab mat dena.”

Lekin iss baar awaaz uski nahi thi.

Woh uski maa ki thi.

—

Chapter Three – The Offerings

Radha mushkil se soti thi. Subah tak, uski aankhein sooj chuki thi aur limbs heavy, lekin raat ke shabd uske sar mein curse ki tarah repeat ho rahe the: maa ki awaaz, warning de rahi thi.

Use convince karna chahta tha ki yeh sapna tha, grief aur monsoon fever se dimaag bigad raha tha. Lekin jab woh verandah par bahar aayi, to sach ne uski saans kat di.

Kuan par latki hui rope abhi bhi geeli thi. Bucket halka sa hil raha tha, khaali. Aur patthar ke rim par, jaise kisi ne carefully rakh diya ho, uski apni diary padi thi — soak ho chuki, pages barbaad, ghar wali diary jaisi hi.

Radha ne kaampati ungliyon se use uthaya. Ink fail chuka tha, shabd dark stains mein blur ho gaye the. Lekin ek line untouched thi, neat, steady handwriting mein:

“The well takes what it wants.”

—

Us subah baad mein, Sita mami wapas aayi, haldi ki jad aur chawal lekar. Usne permission ka intezaar nahi kiya. Purane relatives kabhi nahi karte.

“Tum shaam ka diya nahi jala rahi,” Sita ne kaha, dusty shelves ko scan karte hue frowned. “Tumhari maa kabhi miss nahi karti thi. Agar bukhaar uske jism ko jala raha hota, phir bhi woh jalati thi.”

Radha ne diary table par patak di. “Batao, mami. Woh kuan ko kyun pray karti thi? Usay kya pata tha?”

Sita ke honth seench gaye. Woh khud ko chawal ke dane arrange karne mein busy kar liya. Lambi khamoshi ke baad hi usne jawab diya.

“Purane dinon mein, jab yeh zameen abhi dense jungle thi, kuan yahan nahi tha. Ek pit thi. Log kehte the ki woh naga-lok tak jaati thi, jahan serpents doodh ki jagah aag peete the. Fasal fail hoti thi. Bachche vanish ho jaate the. Elders… woh jo de sakte the de dete the.”

Radha ko apni chamaDi par jalan mehsoos hui. “Offerings? Kis tarah ki?”

Sita ki aankhein akhirkaar uski taraf mudin, tez aur nam. “Naariyal nahi. Phool nahi.”

Radha ka seena tight ho gaya.

“Tumhare daada ji last the jo diye,” Sita ne phuslaaya. “Unke baad, tumhari maa har roz diya jalati thi, taaki kuan phir se na maange. Woh silently burden carry karti thi. Jab woh mari, tumhare pitaji ne ignore kar diya. Aur ab—”

Uski awaaz toot gayi. Usne surprising strength se Radha ki wrist pakdi. “Use wajah mat dena ki tumhe yaad kare.”

Pehle ki Radha kuch keh paati, Sita ne uske haath mein haldi thust kar di aur chali gayi.

—

Us shaam, gaon mandir mein aarti ke liye ikattha hua. Radha kinare khadi, dekh rahi thi ki oil lamps ne thakan aur dar se carved chehron ko roshni di. Chants upar neeche hote rahe, lekin unke neeche, use ek aur awaaz sunai di — jaise underground se uthta hua chorus, awaazein prayer mein nahi, balki bhook mein.

Jab aarti khatam hui, Sundaram phir se uske paas aaya. “Tumhe chhodna chahiye, Radha. Aaj raat, agar ho sake. Kuan restless hai.”

Radha ka gussa, exhaustion se paida hua, ubal pada. “Sab log riddles mein baat karte hain. Meri family yahan rehti thi. Meri maa yahan pray karti thi. Agar main isse tied hoon, to mujhe sach jaanna chahiye. Mere daada ne kya diya? Woh mujhe kis cheez se bacha rahi thi?”

Sundaram ka chehra twitch hua, khamoshi aur farz ke beech phansa hua. Akhirkaar, usne kaha:

“Tumhare daada ne apna firstborn diya.”

Mandir ki ghantiyan tabhi bajin, metallic clangs mein shabd duba diye. Lekin Radha ne kaafi sun liya.

Uski saans ruk gayi. “Firstborn?”

Sundaram ne apna sar jhuka liya. “Tumhara chacha. Tumhare janam se pehle, tumhare pitaji ke bhi aadmi banne se pehle, kuan ne maanga. Aur use feed kiya gaya. Isliye woh tumhara naam jaanta hai, Radha. Isliye tumhari maa darti thi ki woh yaad karega.”

Radha ko zameen ke neeche se tilt hota mehsoos hua. Woh peeche latki, ek brass lamp se takra gayi. Uska flame sputter hua, phir bujh gaya. Ek thandi hawa mandir mein daud gayi, jabki har darwaza band tha.

Kahin door, rope ki cheekh sunai di.

—

Us raat, usne square ki taraf diya jalaya, jaise uski maa ek baar karti thi. Flame wild tarah se flicker kar raha tha, geeli deewaron par shadows phenk raha tha. Radha ne apni diary pakdi hui — asli wali, ruined twin nahi.

Achanak front door par knock hui.

Radha freeze ho gayi. Iss waqt?

Woh cautious kadam se aage badhi. “Kaun hai?”

Koi jawab nahi. Sirf slow drip, drip of water.

Jab usne darwaza khola, koi wahan nahi tha. Sirf chowk faila hua, geela aur chup.

Lekin threshold par ek aur cheez padi thi.

Ek bachche ki payal. Chhoti, chandi ki, jismein ghanti thi jo bajti nahi thi.

Radha ne jhuk kar use uthaya. Jaise hi uski ungliyon ne use chhua, use ek gigglesunai — soft, wet, kuan ki direction se goonjti hui.

Ghar ke andar ka lamp sputter hua aur bujh gaya.

Pehli baar, Radha ne use mehsoos kiya: sirf dar nahi, balki pehchaan.

Kuan sirf use nahi bula raha tha.

Woh use wapas bula raha tha.

—

Chapter Four – The Anklet

Radha ne payal ko wooden table par rakh diya, haath kaamp rahe the. Chandi tarnished thi, umar se dull, lekin tiny bell nayi polish ki tarah chamak rahi thi. Use hila kar dekha — ghanti nahi baji.

Uska pet ulta ho gaya. Sita ke shabd use yaad aaye: Naariyal nahi. Phool nahi.

Kya yeh uske vanish huye chacha ki payal thi? Ya kisi aur bachche ki jo kuan ne le liya tha?

Bahar toofan tez ho gaya, barish tiles par tez-tez gir rahi thi. Ghar uske saath saath saans leta mehsoos ho raha tha — deewarein groan kar rahi thi, darwaze rattling, jaise structure khud jaanta ho ki kya dabav aa raha hai.

Radha ne phir se lamp jalaya. Uska flame violently flicker kar raha tha, deewar par lata hui maa ki tasveer par roshni phenk raha tha. Pehli baar, Radha ne kuch notice kiya jo usne apni poori zindagi miss kar diya tha: maa ke pairon par payal ki faint outline, painting ke background mein blurred, jaise deliberately chhaaya mein chhupa di gayi ho.

Uska gala tight ho gaya.

—

Us raat, woh sapne mein thi — ya sochti thi sapne mein.

Woh kuan ke munh par khadi thi, barefoot, sari barish se chipki hui. Ek rope uske saamne latki hui, pendulum ki tarah hil rahi thi. Neeche bucket chamak raha tha, jabki koi roshni use touch nahi kar rahi thi.

Depth se ek awaaz uthi, iss baar uski nahi, balki ek bachche ki.

“Mujhe yahan mat chhodo.”

Radha ne andar jhaanka. Paani chamak raha tha, aur ek heartbeat ke liye use saaf dikha: ek ladke ka chehra, chamaDi faqa aur swollen, baal jaise weeds tair rahe. Uska chhota haath upar ki taraf pahuncha. Silver payal uske ankle par bandhi hui.

Pehle ki woh chillaa paati, rope ne uske wrist ke gird snap kiya jaise koi living thing ho, aur use aage kheench liya.

Radha jhatke se jaagi, haanpte hue. Uske wrist par rope marks the, raw aur red.

Woh washbasin tak latki, apna chehra splash kiya, aur crack wale mirror mein dekha. Ek second ke liye, use yaqeen hua ki woh uski reflection nahi dekh rahi — aankhein paani se swollen, honth faqe aur phatte hue.

Usne apni muhthi se mirror tod diya.

—

Agli subah, gaon buzzing tha. Pados ke gali se ek ladka raat bhar mein vanish ho gaya tha. Uski maa chowk mein chillaa rahi thi, baal kheench rahi thi, jabki mardon ne kuan se door rehne ki baat ki.

Sundaram ne Radha ko corner kar liya. “Tum dekh rahi ho? Phir se shuru ho gaya hai. Tumhe chhodna chahiye.”

Radha ne sar hilaaya, uski awaaz low aur steady. “Chhodne se bhi nahi rukega. Tum sab jaante ho. Kuan bhoolta nahi.”

Sundaram ka expression collapse ho gaya. “Phir tumhe woh karna chahiye jo tumhari maa karti thi. Diya jalao. Har shaam, bina fail kiye. Bas yehi tareeka hai ise quiet rakhne ka.”

Radha ne hesitation ki. Maa ki ritual repeat karne ka khayal use darawana laga — lekin strangely, resolve bhi diya.

Us shaam, usne phir se diya jalaya, kuan ki taraf. Woh shabd phuslaaye jo usne bachpan se aadhe yaad rakhe the, syllables jo maa ne secret mein murmur kiye the.

Flame steady ho gaya. Ek pal ke liye, hawa shaant mehsoos hui.

Lekin phir, ek bachche ki gigglesquare mein goonji, aur flame bujh gaya, sirf geeli mitti ki boo chhod kar.

—

Us raat, Radha tiny bells ki awaaz se jaagi.

Table par padi payal ring kar rahi thi.

Soft, deliberate chimes kamre mein bhare hue, jabki payal ne pehle kabhi awaaz nahi nikaali thi. Ghanti uneven rhythm mein baj rahi thi, jaise koi chal raha ho, step by step, uske floor par.

Radha freeze ho gayi. Woh payal ko wahan dekh sakti thi jahan chhodi thi — lekin awaaz move kar rahi thi, uske bed ke gird ghoom rahi thi. Invisible feet, geeli aur chhoti, kamre mein chal rahi thi.

Phir steps uske pillow ke paas ruk gaye.

Kuch icy ne uske cheek ko brush kiya.

Radha ne dheere se mud kar dekha, saans ruki hui.

Drowned ladke ka chehra uske apne se inches door hover kar raha tha, sheet par paani tapak raha tha, uske honth khul rahe the bolne ke liye.

“Tumhe wapas aana nahi chahiye tha.”

—

Subah silence layi, lekin Radha ka pillow abhi bhi geela tha. Usne use uthaya, aur uske neeche ek aur diary padi thi, baaki sab jaisi hi. Uske pehle page par sirf ek line thi, maa ki handwriting mein:

“When the well remembers, it comes for blood.”

—

Chapter Five – The Ritual

Radha ne poora subah ghar ko sort karne mein bitaya, kuch bhi dhoondhne ki umeed mein jo maa ne chhod diya ho. Har drawer creek karti, har cupboard dust ugate, lekin puja room mein use woh mila jo usne umeed bhi nahi ki thi: ek chhota iron box, dried neem leaves ke sacks ke neeche chhupa hua.

Andar objects the jo uske pet ko knot kar dete.

Ek clay lamp, saalon ke istemaal se kali hui. Ek coil of red thread, baar-baar bandhne aur kholne se frayed. Aur pages ka bundle, maa ki tight, slanted handwriting mein.

Radha ne padhne se pehle apne paas lamp jalaya.

Pehla page blunt shuru hota tha:

“The well is a mouth. It does not drink water—it drinks memory. If it is not fed, it takes. If it is angered, it takes more. We offer to keep it sleeping.”

Radha ne kaampati haathon se page palta.

“Your father does not believe. He thinks it is old superstition. But I have seen. I saw my brother taken, just as father gave him. I hear the rope creak when there is no wind. I see faces in the water. This house stands because I light the lamp every night. If I stop, it will come for you.”

Letters last page par zyada frantic ho gaye:

“It remembers. Always it remembers.”

—

Radha lambi der tak chup baithi rahi, bahar ki barish clay tiles par hammer kar rahi thi. Maa jaanti thi. Jab Radha bachchi thi, courtyard mein khelti, maa kuan ke saath bargain kar rahi thi, bina explanation ke use protect kar rahi thi.

Ab woh burden us par aagaya tha.

Lekin ek lamp aur whispered words kitni der tak kisi itni purani, itni bhooki cheez ko rok sakti thi?

—

Us shaam, Radha ne decide kiya ki woh ritual ko exactly waisa repeat karegi jaisa maa ne likha tha. Usne red thread apne wrist par bandha, clay lamp jalaya, aur use verandah tak le gayi.

Chowk khaali tha. Gaon walay lambey waqt se apne ghar mein band ho chuke the, doors barrik, jaise unhe pata ho ki raat kisi aur ki hai.

Radha ne lamp ko step par rakh diya, kuan ki taraf. Usne maa ke shabd phuslaaye:

“Sleep, sleep, drink this light, not our blood. Sleep, and do not remember.”

Flame quiver hua. Ek pal ke liye, use laga ki woh bujh jayega, lekin phir woh steady ho gaya, strong aur tall burn karne laga.

Relief ne uske shoulders ko loose kar diya. Woh andar jaane ko mudin.

Phir use sunai di.

Rope ki cheekh.

Slow. Deliberate. Jaise kuan uska intezaar kar raha ho.

Woh wapas mudin, khud ko rok nahi sakti thi. Bucket upar utha, kaala paani tapak raha tha. Lekin rim par settle hone ki bajaye, woh tip hua.

Paani stones par splash hua.

Aur usmein se, kuch crawl kar raha tha.

—

Radha peeche latki. Lamplight mein, use saaf dikha: chhote, faqe haath stones ko pakde hue, ek body upar kheench rahe the — patli, lambi, algae se slick chamaDi. Ek bachcha, aur phir bhi bachcha nahi — neck galat angle par jhuki hui, aankhein paani se cloudy.

Woh uski taraf chaukhoron pe crawl kar raha tha, geeli trail chhodta hua, payal har movement ke saath faintly jingling.

Radha ka gala bandh gaya. Chillana chahta tha, lekin awaaz nahi aayi.

Creature ne steps ke niche ruk kar, swollen lips khol diye.

“Offering kidhar hai?”

—

Lamp ka flame violently flicker hua. Radha ne use pakad liya, haath kaamp rahe the. “Peeche hat!” chillayi.

Creature ne sar tilaya, aankhein firelight reflect kar rahi thi.

“No offering. Phir tum hogi.”

Woh lunge hua.

Radha ne lamp fek diya. Clay toot gaya, oil spill hua, fire geeli zameen par leap kar gayi. Creature chillayi, awaaz high aur broken, phir flames mein collapse kar gayi. Steam hiss hua jab uska jism black water mein dissolve hua, wapas kuan ki taraf seep hua.

Radha knees par gir gayi, dil tez dhadak raha tha.

Chowk phir se chup tha. Sirf rope gently sway kar rahi thi, jaise use mock kar rahi ho.

—

Jab subah aayi, usne steps par payal padi hui payi. Fire se untouched.

Usne use uthaya aur phuslaaya, “Tum mujhse kya chahte ho?”

Kuan ki depth se faintest jawab aaya, morning breeze par lehrata hua:

“Blood.”

—

Chapter Six – The Hungry Village

Payal Radha ki palm mein softly clink kar rahi thi. Woh use ghoor rahi thi, pulse uske kaanon mein thrum kar rahi thi. Kuan ne bola tha. Riddles mein nahi, dreams mein mistaken whispers nahi — clear, demanding, undeniable.

Blood.

Jab tak suraj uncha chadha, Radha abhi bhi verandah par baithi thi, sari raat ki barish se geeli. Gaon walay tezi se guzarte, unki nazarin sharp, almost hostile.

Pehli baar, Radha ne notice kiya: koi bhi dusra kuan ke paas nahi aata tha. Woh uske moss-dark stones se door rehte, jaise priests polluted shrine se bachte hain. Aur phir bhi — use fill karne ki baat nahi karte, cover karne ki himmat nahi karte. Woh simply… let it be.

Tabhi use ehsaas hua: sirf uski family hi usse bound nahi thi. Pure gaon ka complicity tha.

—

Dopahar tak, Sundaram aaya, white dhoti par keechad ke chhinte. Chehra pehle se bhi zyada budha, drawn aur thakan bhara.

“Tumne dekha,” usne greeting ke bina kaha.

Radha ne sar hilaaya. Uska gala sukha hua tha. “Usne offering maangi.”

Uska jaw tighten hua. “Phir woh sachmuch jaag gaya hai. Humare paas sirf ek choice hai.”

Radha ki awaaz gusse se kaamp rahi thi. “Tum iska matlab kisi ko dena chahte ho. Phir se.”

Sundaram ne deny nahi kiya. “Yeh gaon balance se survive karta hai. Jab woh maange, hum dete hain. Jab woh khata hai, barsaat hamari fasal ko bless karti hai. Jab hum inkar karte hain…” Woh ruka, uske fields ki taraf dekha. “Last time jab kisi ne resist kiya, cholera aaya. Poori families hafte mein rot gayi. Hum itne strong nahi hain ki ise fight kar sake, Radha.”

Radha ka pet ulta ho gaya. “Aur tum sab bas—accept kar lete ho?”

Usne gravely dekha. “Tum samajhti nahi. Sirf hum nahi jo ise feed karte hain. Yahan se lekar purane jungle tak har gaon ka apna bargain hai. Kothivaram ka burden yeh kuan hai.”

—

Us raat, whispers storm winds se tez fail gaye. Radha ko apne kadam uthane par bhi nazaron ka weight mehsoos hota. Water pump par auratein jab woh guzarti to chatter ruk jaati. Tea stall ke paas ikatthe mardon ki awaazein neeche ho jaati.

Akhirkaar, Sita mami uske darwaze par aayi. Chehra pale, honth kaamp rahe the.

“Woh kehte hain ki tumhe hona chahiye,” Sita ne phuslaaya. “Tumhara khoon. Tumhari family isse bound hai. Agar gaon tumhe de dega, to baaki safe rahenge.”

Radha ka seena jal raha tha. “Aur tum unse agree karti ho?”

Budhi aurat ki aankhon mein aansoo aaye. “Beta, agar tum khudko nahi dogi, to woh tumhe le lenge. Main unhe rok nahi sakti.”

—

Us raat, Radha ne doors aur windows bolt kar diye. Lekin neend nahi aayi. Hawa khud heavy thi, expectation se thick.

Kahin door, drums beat karne lage. Slow, steady, ritualistic.

Woh window tak chupke se gayi. Chowk figures se bhara hua tha torches lekar. Men, women, even children — chehre sakht, aankhein vacant. Woh kuan ke gird circle bana kar chant kar rahe the. Firelight ne unke features ko grotesque masks bana diye.

Rope phir se move hui, jabki koi touch nahi kar raha tha. Bucket upar utha, black water stones par tapakaya.

Aur phir chanting ruk gayi.

Ek saath, har villager ne apna sar uski taraf mudaya.

Radha window se peeche latki, dil tez dhadak raha tha. Use unke footsteps sunai diye, sauon ki tarah, stones par geeli, uske darwaze ki taraf aate hue.

Usne payal pakdi aur tight clutch ki. “Tumhe khoon chahiye?” usne phuslaaya, awaaz phatti. “Phir mere mine choke ho jaaoge, main freely nahi doongi.”

Darwaza rattled. Sauon haathon ne ek saath use strike kiya.

Aur kuan se, pehle se bhi zyada loud, maa ki awaaz aayi.

“Radha… darwaza kholo.”

—

Chapter Seven: The Bloodline Bargain

Monsoon ke badal horizon ko bruised purple shades mein doobaa chuke the. Radha gaon ke chowk mein khadi thi, sari mist se geeli jo zameen se curl kar rahi thi. Uske gird, villagers ek ring bana kar khade the — silent, watchful, unki nazarin us aur temple priest Pandit Somnath ke beech dart rahi thi. Hawa heavy thi, jaise monsoon winds bhi is circle of accusation ko cross karne se darein.

“Tumne kuan ko gussa dilaya hai, Radha,” Somnath ki awaaz thunder ki tarah giri, trembling haath mein sanctified water ka brass vessel pakda hua. “Tumhare wapas aane ke baad, zameen bigad gayi hai. Mawaashi beemar pad rahe hain, fasal harvest se pehle rot rahi hai, aur bachche drowning ke sapne dekh rahe hain. Tumne kuch hila diya jo kabhi touch nahi hona chahiye tha.”

Radha ke honth kaamp rahe the, lekin uski awaaz steady rahi. “Main nahi hoon. Yeh kuan khud hai. Hamesha se kuan raha hai.”

Crowd mein unease ka ripple gaya. Villagers ne ek doosre ki taraf dekha, prayers ke fragments whisper kiye, lekin koi zor se baat nahi karne ki himmat ki. Sirf Somnath ki aankhein purane, thake hue gusse se jalti thi.

“Kya tum sochte ho ki tum unse zyada jaanti ho jo tumse pehle aaye? Har family ne us kuan mein offerings daali hain, Radha. Saadiyon se. Isliye hum jeete hain, hamari fasal ugti hai, ham famine nahi jhelte jaise aas paas ke gaon. Woh bhook tumhari nahi jo tum question kar sakti ho.”

Radha ka gala tight ho gaya. “Phir bhi woh kyun leta hai? Raaton ko cries kyun sunai deti hain? Mere maa ko kyun liya?”

Hush chha gaya. Radha ki maa ka naam — Kamala — chowk mein forbidden word ki tarah hang kar gaya. Somnath ka expression flicker hua, fear dikhaya phir control mein aa gaya.

Radha ne ek kadam aage badhaya. “Tumne mujhe sach nahi bataya. Tumne kaha woh flood mein kho gayi. Lekin mujhe pata hai. Mujhe pata hai woh kuan ko di gayi. Just like others before her.”

Crowd se gasps nikle. Ek aurat ne apne bachche ko aur pakad liya, jaise dar rahi ho ki mention se hi woh sacrifice ke liye mark ho jaye.

Somnath ki awaaz neeche gir gayi, soft lekin cutting. “Kamala chosen thi. Usse nahi liya gaya. Usne apna farz samjha, tumhari tarah nahi. Isliye tum bachpan mein bach gayi. Kyunki tumhare ghar se already khoon diya ja chuka tha.”

Radha ko zameen ke neeche se tilt hota mehsoos hua. Maa jaanti thi? Agree karti thi? Kamala ki soft smile, shaam ko phuslaayi gayi lullabies, yeh grotesque revelation se clash kar rahi thi.

Edge par khada ek patla, reedy aadmi akhirkaar bola. “Hamesha same families, Somnathji. Mere daada ne bhi yehi kaha tha. Same names, phir se phir se.”

Priest ne sharply mud kar dekha. “Chup!” Lekin shabd ab flow kar rahe the, bottled fear ke saal unhe carry kar rahe the.

“Haan,” ek aur awaaz uthi, trembling lekin fierce. “Meri maa kehti thi ki kuan sabko nahi bhookta — woh choose karta hai. Woh khud ko blood se bind karta hai. Aur woh families pay karti hain.”

Radha ka seena uchal raha tha. “Bloodlines,” usne phuslaaya. “Offerings ka sawaal hi nahi hai. Woh families ko mark karta hai… jaise ek curse.”

Somnath ka mask of authority crack ho gaya. “Tum ise fight nahi kar sakti, Radha. Kuan ka claim gaon se bhi purana hai, hum sab se bhi purana. Tumhari maa ki bloodline bound hai. Aur ab tum ho.”

Uske kaan goonj rahe the. Villagers ke whispers well ki raaton ki remembered cries se merge ho rahe the. Achanak, woh clearer, closer lag rahe the — jaise kin paani se pukar rahe hon.

Radha peeche latki, pet mein bile uth raha tha. Dadi ki kahaaniyaan, elders ka kuan ke baare mein sawaal karne par chup kar dena — sab is bargain ka hissa tha — families doomed to feed it with their blood.

Lekin fear ke neeche jo claw kar raha tha, ek aur emotion jag rahi thi. Rage.

“Tum humein silence se bound rakhte ho,” usne kaha, awaaz low lekin searing. “Tum ise duty kehte ho. Lekin yeh ek chain hai. Meri maa ne jhuk kar accept kiya hoga, lekin main nahi karungi.”

Somnath ne brass vessel ko uncha uthaya, aankhein feverish. “Ise defy karo, aur pure gaon ko suffer karna parega! Ek jaan kayi ke liye. Yehi balance hai. Torn karo, aur sab drown ho jayenge famine aur sickness mein.”

Radha ki nazar crowd par gayi. Chehre — thake hue, darre hue, resigned. Use mothers dikhi jo bachchon ko pakde hue, men calloused hands se, young girls jo wide, silent dread se use dekh rahi thi. Woh bhi is ancient hunger ke prisoners the, jaise woh.

“Nahi,” usne kaha, awaaz ne air ko crack kiya. “Ek jaan kayi ke liye nahi. Hamari silence ne ise hamare khoon se zyada feed kiya hai. Aur agar ise tootna hai, to main hi toodungi.”

Villagers piche hat gaye jaise usne blasphemy bola ho. Somnath ka chehra terror aur fury se distort hua.

Aur phir, faintly, kuan ki direction se, storm-laden hawa mein ek awaaz carry hui — gurgling sigh, jaise deep, black paani se uthi hansi.

Radha ne tab samjha: kuan ne use sun liya tha.

Aur woh itni aasaan nahi chhodega.

—

Chapter Eight: The Well’s Whisper

Barish raat tak puri tarah shuru ho chuki thi, clay walls par lash kar rahi thi. Lanterns doorways mein weakly flicker kar rahe the, lekin zyadatar gaon andar huddled, thunder ke roll par prayers whisper kar rahe the.

Radha, bone tak geeli, akeli kuan ki taraf chal rahi thi. Uski sari skin se chipki hui, mud uske pairon ko har kadam par swallow kar rahi thi. Peeche, doors softly shut ho rahe the, jaise villagers khud ko uski defiance se seal kar rahe hon.

Kuan tak ka rasta storm mein chamak raha tha. Har lightning flash ne banyan tree ko illuminate kiya jo paani ke upar jhuka hua tha, uski roots skeletal fingers ki tarah earth ko pakde hue. Radha ne temple se churai hui brass lamp pakdi hui, apne haath se uske chhote flame ko shield kar rahi thi.

Jitna paas aayi, utna loud hua — paani ki awaaz, haan, lekin layered kuch aur ke saath. Whispers. Sirf distant wails nahi jo use hamesha sunai deti thi, balki words. Fragments, uth kar girte, jaise awaazein uske kaan ke paas press ho rahi hon.

“Radha…”

Uski saans ruk gayi. Maa ki awaaz thi.

Woh aage latki, dil tez dhadak raha tha. Kuan uske saamne loom kar raha tha, mossy stones barish se geeli. Usne jhuk kar dekha, lamp trembling grip mein. Flame dance kar raha tha, neeche black water swirling reveal kar raha tha.

“Ma?” usne phuslaaya, gala tight.

Paani ripple hua jaise andar se stir hua ho. Aur phir, impossibly, maa ka chehra surface par shimmer hua — aankhein wide, honth sorrow se kaamp rahe.

“Radha, beti… tumhe aana nahi chahiye tha.”

Radha ki knees buckle ho gayi. Aansoo uski vision blur kar rahe the, barish se mix ho rahe the. “Unhone mujhe jhooth bola. Unhone kaha tum chali gayi. Unhone tumhe—”

Maa ki reflection shudder hui, awaaz fractured glass ki tarah toot gayi. “Maine choose kiya, Radha. Kuan humein bind karta hai. Mera turn tha. Aur ab tumhara hai.”

“Nahi!” Radha ne apne haath se stone rim ko slam kiya. “Main nahi karungi! Main tumhari tarah swallow nahi hone dunga. Main is curse ko feed nahi karungi!”

Chehra dissolve hua, dozens se replace hua — men, women, children — drowned phantoms ki tarah in-out flicker kar rahe. Unke munh ek saath move kar rahe the, whispering in unison.

“Bound… bound… khoon wapas aana chahiye…”

Radha peeche latki, slick stones par slip hone wali. Uske kaan roar kar rahe the unke chant se. Paani violently churn hua, uske chehre par icy droplets spray kiye.

“Hum kyun?” usne storm mein chillaya. “Sirf hum kyun? Tumhe kya chahiye?”

Lightning ne aasman split kiya, aur ek instant ke liye, kuan ki depth glow hui — paani nahi, balki ek cavernous mouth, stone ki teeth se, endlessly neeche tak. Villagers ki awaazein galat thi. Hunger nahi tha jo kuan ko uski family se bind karta tha. Possession thi.

Bloodline sirf debt nahi thi — tether thi. Har chosen sacrifice disappear nahi hota tha. Woh swallow ho jaata tha, kisi half-existence mein kept, cheez ki strength feed karta tha.

Radha knees par gir gayi, lamp pakdi hui. Whispers ab screams mein badal gaye, maa ki awaaz sabse upar:

“Radha, agar tum resist karti ho, to sirf tumhe nahi lega. Sabko lega.”

Thunder crash hua. Banyan tree ki roots vein ki tarah quiver hui jo kuan ko feed kar rahi thi.

Uska dimaag tezi se daud raha tha. Agar woh surrender karti, cycle continue hoga — uski bloodline forever bound. Agar fight karti, pure gaon ko risk karegi.

Lekin pehli baar, Radha ko clarity mehsoos hui. Use samajh aaya ki maa ne kyun jhuka, itne logon ne kyun chup rehna choose kiya. Fear tha. Fear of breaking what centuries ago set ho chuka tha.

Aur Radha ka rage fear se zyada tez jal raha tha.

Woh uthi, lamp ko uncha pakdi. “Phir use mujhe le lo,” usne kaha, awaaz steady. “Lekin main quietly nahi jaungi. Bound nahi jaungi. Main ise andar se tear open karungi.”

Hawa howl hui, almost mocking. Paani surge hua, massive splash ne uski taraf reach kiya jaise kuan khud use pakadne ki koshish kar raha ho. Flame sputter hua, nearly bujh gaya, lekin stubbornly cling kar gaya.

Radha ne stone rim par kadam rakha.

Peeche, faint movement ne uski nazar pakdi. Ek group villagers — torches lekar men, infants ko pakde hue women — door se follow kar rahe the. Unke chehre terror se glow kar rahe the, lekin kuch aur bhi: desperation. Woh dekhne aaye the, ensure karne ki woh willingly kuan mein jaaye.

Somnath ki awaaz storm mein carry hui. “Hona hi hai, Radha! Tumhari bloodline owe karti hai. Agar tum inkar karti ho, to tum sabko condemn karti ho!”

Radha ne mud kar dekha, baal chehre se chipke hue. “Phir tum sab khud ko condemn karte ho. Kyunki main is curse ko nahi jhukungi. Aaj raat, yeh khatam hoga.”

Crowd se gasps uthin. Somnath aage badha, aankhein blazing. Lekin bolne se pehle, kuan roared — thunder se deeper, earth se older. Zameen unke pairon ke neeche shake hui.

Banyan tree crack hui, roots serpent ki tarah free tearing. Villagers chillaye, scatter huye, jabki Radha rim ko pakde hue.

Kuan fully awaken ho gaya tha.

Aur usko khoon chahiye tha.

—

Chapter Nine: The Drowning Mouth

Duniya kuan ki roar se split ho gayi.

Mud Radha ke pairon ke neeche slide hua jab rim crack hua, shards of stone abyss mein gir gaye. Banyan ki roots, moti aur glistening jaise serpents, storm mein whip karti hui, kuan ke lip ke gird coil hui, jaise earth ko hold karne ki koshish kar rahi hon.

Aur phir, ek root ne strike kiya.

Woh Radha ke ankle ke gird wet snap ke saath coil hui, use aage kheench liya. Woh chillayi, lamp fekne ki koshish ki, lekin flame ground tak pahunchne se pehle gutter out ho gaya.

Villagers shadows mein chillaye, kuch knees par gir gaye prayer mein, kuch rain mein bhaag gaye. Somnath nahi hila. Woh apni torch uncha uthaye khada tha, aankhein fervor se blazing — faith se kam, fear given shape se zyada.

“Use le lo!” usne storm mein bellow kiya. “Woh tumhara hai!”

Root ne zor se kheencha. Radha ne stone rim ko claw kiya, nails moss aur slick mud se rip huye. Uska jism aage tip hua, kuan ka black mouth neeche endless throat ki tarah gape kar raha tha.

Paani ki roar shift hui — storm jaisi nahi, balki voices ka chorus. Screams. Laughs. Pleas. Woh uske gird wrap hui, uske skull ko bhare hue. Use phir se maa ki awaaz sunai di, faint lekin clear:

“Use bind hone mat dena, Radha. Agar gir jaogi, to ise fight karna. Fight karna!”

Root ne violently jerk kiya, aur woh plunge kar gayi.

Thand ne usko ek heartbeat mein swallow kar liya. Icy darkness ne uske lungs crush kar diye, kaan paani aur voices ki pounding se bhare. Woh flail karti rahi, lekin roots use deeper, deeper, endless throat mein spiral kar rahe the.

Aur phir — light.

Sunlight nahi, balki pale, greenish glow, cavern walls ki cracks se oozing. Radha ka jism well ke neeche vast chamber mein break through hua, stone aur bone se carved cathedral. Deewarein faintly pulse kar rahi thi, slick jaise zinda. Unmein faces swim kar rahe the, half-formed, munh eternal scream mein stretch — uske ancestors, kuan ki flesh mein trapped.

Center mein jagged rock ka altar tha, aur uspar… kuch.

Insan nahi. Beast nahi. Paani aur stone ka hulking figure, form shifting jaise clay, chest par faces forming aur dissolving. Uske eyes nahi the, lekin woh use dekh raha tha. Uska munh khula — endless cavern, bone ki teeth se lined.

Uske gird ki awaazein chup ho gayi. Aur phir, ek saath, woh uske through bole.

“Radha. Bloodline. Tum wapas aayi ho.”

Uska jism kaampa, lekin uski awaaz break hui. “Tumne saadiyon se humse liya hai. Meri maa. Meri kin. Tum humein bind karte ho, humein feed karte ho. Kyu?”

Entity ripple hui, closer shift hui. Dobara jab bole, deewarein quiver hui.

“Tum anchor ho. Ek line, chosen. Bina khoon ke, zameen marr jati hai. Bina sacrifice ke, famine failta hai. Tumhari kin ne agree kiya. Unki jaan duniya ki saans ke badle.”

Radha ka pet ulta ho gaya. “Ek bargain. Curse nahi — bargain jo mere pehle walon ne kiya.”

Figure looming, black water tapak raha. “Bargain sealed. Torn karna… tumhe unka place lena hoga.”

Maa ka chehra altar ke peeche wall par flicker hua, aankhein hollow lekin pleading. “Radha… surrender mat karna. Crack dhoondho. Use tear open karo.”

Radha ki muhthi bhandi. Ab use samajh aaya — cycle sacred nahi, prison tha. Pact fear mein likha, silence se feed hua. Aur woh, apni bloodline ki last, end karne ka ek chance rakhti thi.

Entity surge hui, altar se roots rise hui, uski taraf slam hui. Woh side roll hui, haathon ko stone par raw kar liya. Uske lungs air ke liye chillaye, lekin khud ko upright force kiya, aankhein deewaron ko scan kar rahi. Aur phir use dikha — ek opening, faint, jahan paani gushing, sickly glow disrupt kar raha tha. Weak point.

Woh daudi.

Roots lashed, legs ko tear kar rahi, waist ko wrap kar rahi. Woh paani mein chillayi, clawing, inch-by-inch aage pull karti rahi. Maa ki awaaz louder uthi, hundreds ancestors ke saath — stone mein bound.

“Break karo, Radha! Break karo!”

Uski nails ne crack ko dig kiya. Rock living flesh ki tarah pulse kar rahi thi, resisting. Woh anyway tear karti rahi, searing pain ko ignore karti hui jab blood paani ko cloud kar gaya. Entity roar hui, munh wide, chamber shake hua jaise duniya convulse ho rahi ho.

Aur phir —

Crack split hua.

Black water ka torrent blast hua, use backward sweep kiya. Entity shriek hui, form unravel hua, faces nothingness mein dissolve. Deewarein scream hui jab trapped spirits current mein surge huye.

Radha upward sweep hui, spinning, lungs burning, collapsing stone aur shrieking spirits ki roar uske sar mein. Woh kicked, clawed, surface tak pahunchne ki dua maangi pahle ki andhera use le le.

Upar, faintly, use storm ki lightning well ke circle mein dikhi.

Woh uski taraf surge hui.

Surface break karne se pehle last jo suna, woh maa ki awaaz thi, soft aur fierce:

“Tum pehli ho jo ise defy karti ho. Jeena.”

—

Chapter Ten: The Flooded Silence

Radha kuan se corpse ki tarah saans lene wapas aayi, coughing aur gasping jab barish uske chehre par lash kar rahi thi. Usne cracked rim ko claw kiya, mossy stones ke upar khud ko drag kiya. Banyan ki roots limp ho chuki thi, shrunken, jaise unka life suck ho chuka ho.

Villagers, jo trembling silence mein ikatthe the, uske dekh kar piche hat gaye. Kuan mein ghisde hue ladki kabhi wapas nahi aati. Kabhi nahi. Lekin yahan thi Radha — zinda, bleeding, aur wild-eyed.

Lambi, shuddering moment tak, koi nahi bola.

Phir Pandit Somnath aage badha, torch abhi bhi storm ke bawajood burning. Chehra ashen, lekin awaaz accusation se sharp.

“Tumne kya kiya?”

Radha stagger kar ke uthi, sari torn, skin blood aur mud se streaked. Uski awaaz rasp kar rahi thi, lekin nahi tooti.

“Maine ise toda.”

Villagers gasp karte hue apne aap ko cross karne lage. Bachche roye, women ne unhe pakda. Somnath ki aankhein terror ke kareeb wide hui.

“Bewakoof ladki! Yeh bargain saadiyon se hamari raksha kar raha tha. Yehi tha jo humein famine, plague, aur drought se bachata tha. Bina iske, kya tum sochti ho hum survive karenge? Kya tum sochti ho duniya humein freely feed karegi?”

Radha ne sar hilaaya, barish uske tangled hair se tapak rahi thi. “Usne humein kabhi bachaya nahi. Woh humein feed kar raha tha. Hum iske bawajood survive karte the, iski wajah se nahi.”

Lekin jab woh yeh keh rahi thi, uske gut mein unease katta. Storm ab louder tha — unnaturally. Deep, guttural rumble thunder ke neeche roll kar raha tha, jaise kuch ancient jag raha ho.

Zameen tremble hui. Villagers chillaye, ek doosre ko pakde hue jab earth banyan ke base par split hui. Black water geyser ki tarah upar erupt hua, crowd ko drench kar diya. Iron aur rot ki boo aayi.

Somnath knees par gir gaya, torch deluge mein hiss karke bujh gayi. “Tumne hum sabko doom kar diya,” usne rote hue kaha. “Kuan ki bhook ab bound nahi hai. Tumne ise free kar diya.”

Radha ka seena tight ho gaya. Free. Shabd uske skull mein echo hua. Use tether toda tha, haan — lekin shayad tether sirf uski bloodline ke liye nahi tha. Shayad woh entity ko contain karne wali ek cheez thi.

Villagers phir se chillaye jab geyser twist hui, towering column of water ban gayi. Faces usmein flicker kar rahe the, wohi faces jo Radha ne neeche cavern mein dekhi thi. Lekin ab woh trapped nahi the — woh upward spill ho rahe the, storm mein dissolve ho rahe the.

Kuch roye. Kuch chillaye. Aur kuch… hanste the.

Column burst hui, ek wave chowk mein crash hui. Huts topple hui, clay walls collapse hui. Log mud mein pheinke gaye, safety ke liye scramble kar rahe the.

Radha ne kuan ke stones ke paas brace kiya, dil tez dhadak raha tha. Use unhe free karna tha — maa, ancestors. Lekin freedom ke teeth the.

Chaos ke beech, use phir se maa ki awaaz sunai di, faint, jaise rain par carry hui whisper.

“Radha… bhaago.”

Uska khoon thanda ho gaya. Ruko nahi. Ladho nahi. Bhaago.

Usne villagers ki taraf dekha, unke chehre terror aur fury se twist. Kuch use savior ki tarah dekh rahe the, kuch curse in flesh. Somnath ne shaking hand se uski taraf point kiya.

“Pakdo use!” usne chillaya, spittle flying. “Usne ant unleash kar diya!”

Kuch men aage badhe, desperation ne fear ko override kar diya. Radha peeche latki, mud uske ankles ko pakde hue. Uski aankhein banyan ke paar fields ki taraf dartin, ab storm ki violent glow mein shimmering.

Use tab samajh aaya: battle ab villagers se nahi thi. Priest se bhi nahi.

Kuch kuan se utha tha. Aur woh ab bargains, na bloodlines se bound tha.

Radha ne mud kar bhaag diya.

Peeche, village screams aur paani mein dissolve ho gaya.

—

Chapter Eleven: The Water That Followed

Radha barefoot fields mein daudi, barish uske chehre par slash kar rahi thi, mud uske kadam pakde hue. Peeche, village shrieks aur crashing water mein dissolve ho raha tha. Banyan tree thunder ki tarah earth tear karne ki awaaz ke saath topple hui, uski roots thrash kar phir vanish kar gayi churning flood mein.

Woh tab tak nahi ruki jab tak cries faint nahi ho gayi, rain ki steady hiss se replace ho gayi. Uske lungs burn kar rahe the, legs kaamp rahe the, lekin woh aage force karti rahi, paddy fields jo lakes ban chuki thin, narrow paths jo torrents of black water mein drown ho chuki thin.

Akhirkaar, woh road ke paas half-fallen shrine ke shelter tak pahunchi. Uska stone idol, erosion se beyond recognition, sideways lean kar raha tha jaise storm se escape karne ki koshish kar raha ho. Radha collapse hui, chest heaving.

Uske haath kaamp rahe the. Blood abhi bhi cuts se ooze kar raha tha jo usne cavern wall ko claw karte hue kiye the. Red barish mein tezi se spread hua, zameen mein vanish ho gaya.

Ek pal ke liye, use laga woh akeli hai. Lekin phir — use sunai di.

Drip.

Barish nahi. Yeh slower, deliberate tha. Shrine ke andar hi steady patter.

Radha freeze ho gayi, mud kar dekha.

Stone idol ki chest ki cracks se paani seep ho raha tha. Thick, black, iron ki boo aayi. Woh base par pool hua, uske knees ki taraf spread hua.

“No…” usne phuslaaya. “Yeh nahi ho sakta…”

Woh bahar ki taraf latki, lekin uska pet gira. Road khud wet thi — barish ka paani nahi, balki wohi black seepage, soil se trickling jaise zameen bleed kar rahi ho. Woh paths par slither kar rahi thi, fields ki taraf, horizon ki taraf.

Curse gaya nahi tha. Woh spread ho raha tha.

Radha ne apna sar pakda, panic uth raha tha. Bargain toda gaya tha, haan — lekin ise tod kar, use apni prison se loose kar diya tha. Ab usko uski bloodline ki zaroorat nahi thi. Woh zameen khud se peena chahta tha.

Door, faint storm ke through, lanterns bobbing dikhe. Ek group villagers, soaked aur staggering, dhoondh rahe the. Use.

Uska instinct chhipne ko chillaya, lekin ek aur thought sharper cut kiya: Agar woh yeh paani dekhenge, to jaanenge ki famine se bhi bura hai. Jaanenge ki Somnath ne jhooth bola tha.

Woh tall grass mein chup gayi. Villagers paas aaye, unki awaazein storm ke upar ragged.

“Woh hum sabko cursed kar diya—”

“Nahi! Priest ne kaha tha ki kuan hamara shield tha—”

“Shield toot chuka hai, kya tum nahi dekh rahe?”

Radha ki saans ruk gayi. Unke dar ke through bhi, ab doubt tha. Somnath ki authority mein cracks.

Lekin doubt alone usse nahi rok sakta jo unleash ho chuka tha.

Woh grass mein aur creep hui, sochne ki koshish kar rahi. Entity ek baar bound thi. Bargains ki power thi. Agar pact se pehle trap hui thi, to shayad phir se bind ki ja sakti thi — lekin blood se nahi. Kuch aur se.

Uske thoughts daud rahe the, rushing water ki awaaz se clash kar rahe the jo jahan bhi woh kadam rakhti thi follow karti thi. Black seepage ab grass ke through thread ho chuki thi, tez ho rahi thi, almost use dhoondh rahi thi.

Aur phir, storm ke through, use dikha — ek purani cart road jungle ki taraf winding. Jungle ke paar ruins of old fort, jahan uski dadi ne bachpan mein jaane se mana kiya tha. Woh kehte the ki uske dungeons mein kuch tha jo village se bhi purana tha. Kuch jiske bare mein priest bhi kabhi nahi bola.

Dadi ki awaaz memory mein echo hui: “Well se pehle shrines the, Radha. Shrines jo ab ke gods se bhi purane the.”

Radha ne barish ko apni aankhon se wipe kiya, uska decision harden hua. Agar woh jo free hua tha usse fight karna chahti thi, to answers wahan the — ruins mein, forgotten shrines of those who first bargained with the hunger.

Usne apni torn sari ko tighten kiya aur jungle ki taraf mud gayi.

Peeche, villagers ki shouts uthin, ab screams se mingle jab black water fields se spill hui aur unke heels tak surge hui.

Hunger ab patient nahi tha. Woh sab kuch le raha tha.

—

Chapter Twelve: The Ruins of Forgotten Bargains

Radha ne ghaney jungle mein apna raasta banaya, baarish ki behtaab boondon ne uske sar pe thappad maara. Kichad uske pairon se chipka hua tha, daaliyan uski sari ko phaad rahi thi, lekin woh ruki nahi. Kaali pani peeche-piche chal raha tha, jungle ke floor par raengta hua, jadon ke beech ikattha hota, saans leti hui si siskari bharta hua — jaise zinda ho.

Aakhirkaar, woh aakhri jhaadiyon se nikli aur khandahar ke saamne khadi ho gayi. Purana qila toofan mein daraavna sa uchhal raha tha, belon aur kaai ne aadha nigal liya tha. Uske pathar ke deewaron par umr ka kaala daag pada tha — girne waale magar ajeeb taur pe tikaye hue. Bijli chamki, tootey hue battlements aur giray hue mehrebon ko roshni mein dikhaya — ek purana qila jo sau saalon ke khoon aur dar ko dekhta aaya tha.

Radha ki saans phool rahi thi. Uski maa ke dabe hue darr ke warnings uske kaanon mein goonj rahe the. “Kuan purana hai, lekin usse bhi purane hain shrines. Woh pehle kiye gaye bargains ko yaad rakhte hain.”

Woh dheere se aage badhi, kaala pani jaise zinda parchhaai uske peeche latka hua. Main gate zang lagay hinges par tiltila rahi thi. Andar, courtyard weeds aur toote hue pattharon se bhara pada tha. Beech mein ek platform khada tha — ek hi granite slab se kata hua, aise symbols se bhara hua jo usne kabhi nahi dekhe the.

Jab woh paas pahunchi, baarish thodi kam ho gayi, jaise silence ke aage sa respect kar rahi ho. Storm ki gray light mein symbols halki si chamak rahi thi. Usne unpar ungliyon pherayi, patterns ko trace kiya. Woh uski chhoo ke neeche pulse kar rahe the — garam, jaise saans le rahe hon.

Ek awaaz — purani, gehri, jaisi kabhi suni na ho — patthar se uthi. Dhamki nahi, cruel bhi nahi, lekin dhyan maangne waali.

“Radha. Tum aa gayi ho. Kya tum sach jaanna chahti ho?”

Uska dil dhadka. “Haan,” usne phusphusaya. “Mujhe jaanna hai ki ise kaise roka jaaye.”

Patthar ne phir pulse kiya. Symbols pani ki tarah lehron ki tarah shift hone lage. “Yeh kuan bhookepan se paida hua tha, lekin bhookepan hamesha apna nahi tha. Bahut pehle, gaon se pehle, tumhare khoon ke line se pehle, duniya pacts se chalti thi. Pani agreements peeta tha, maans nahi. Woh pehle bargains trust aur respect ke the. Waqt ke saath, greed ne line ko poison kar diya. Khoon ne trust ko replace kar diya. Sacrifice ne consent ko hataya. Isi tarah kuan ab woh cheez chahne laga jo ab chahata hai.”

Radha ne thithuri li. “Toh yeh sirf meri family nahi hai. Yeh gaon hai. Pure Kothivaram ka mamla hai.”

“Haan. Aur agar balance restore nahi kiya gaya, toh woh sabko kha jayega.”

“Balance?” Uski awaaz tez hui, gala ruk gaya. “Main ise kaise restore karun? Bhookepan ko kaise rokun?”

Patthar uski ungliyon ke neeche saans leta hua sa lag raha tha. “Jo liya gaya, wapas lao — khoon se nahi, samajh se. Memory offer karo, acknowledgement, aur un logon ke naam jo suffer kiye. Sirf debt ko naam dekar hi bhookepan ko undo kiya ja sakta hai.”

Radha ka dimaag ghoom gaya. Memory, names… usko samajh aaya. Gaon ne sau saal tak apne aap ko mute kar rakha tha. Secrets aur fear ne kuan ko khoon ke lines se zyada der tak feed kiya tha. Use azad karne ke liye, use gaon ko sach ke saamne laana padega, silence ko todna padega.

Bijli ne aasmaan phaad diya, ruins ko roshni mein dikhaya. Kaala pani, jo gaon se nikal kar jungle se guzra tha, qile ke courtyard mein ghus gaya. Hiss karta hua, toofan se mara hua, lekin pehli baar, usne Radha pe attack nahi kiya. Woh intezaar kar raha tha.

Radha ne gehri saans li. Uske haath kaamp rahe the jab woh platform pe chadhi, apni awaaz ko baarish ke upar uthaya.

“Main tumhe dekh rahi hoon!” usne chillaya. “Main dekhti hoon woh bhookepan jo tumne saha, woh bargains jo dar mein kiye gaye! Main un families ke naam leti hoon jo tumhe feed ki, jo bacche kho gaye, jo maaen roti rahi, jo pita ne duaen maangi! Main unke suffering ko ab aur chhupne nahi doongi!”

Stone platform zyada chamakne laga, symbols glow kar rahe the jaise approve kar rahe hon. Kaala pani thoda piche hata, ghoomta hua, hiss karta hua, lekin attack nahi kiya. Upar ka toofan jaise ruk gaya.

Radha ki chhati jal rahi thi, lekin woh bolti rahi. Naam uske muh se giren, ek ke baad ek, jab tak pani ki garaj ek tremor mein badal gayi jo poore ruins ko hila diya. Har naam jo usne bola, har memory jo usne acknowledge ki, kaale pani ka zameen par pakad halka padta gaya.

Aur phir — silence.

Baarish dheemi ho gayi. Kaala pani shaant ho gaya. Platform ke symbols fade ho gaye, patthar thanda par shaant reh gaya.

Radha ghenon par baith gayi, thak kar. Apni zindagi mein pehli baar, usko laga ki shayad bhookepan satisfy ho gaya ho — na khoon se, na dar se, balki sach se.

Kuan, kahin door neeche, chup tha.

Abhi ke liye.

—

Chapter Thirteen: Reckoning in Kothivaram

Radha subah ke waqt Kothivaram laut aayi. Toofan guzar chuka tha, gaon geela aur khamoshi mein kapta hua chhod gaya tha. Har jagah kichad chipka hua tha; chhaten gir chuki thi, aur kaala pani pichhade hua, sadkon aur kheton par kaale, tel jaise nishaan chhod gaya tha.

Woh seedhe square mein dakhil hui. Banyan tree bilkul gir chuka tha, jadon ka koi faida nahi, kichad mein bejaan pari hui. Kuan shaant baitha tha, uski rope dhalakayi hui, bucket bechaini se kinare par pada hua.

Gaon walay darwaazon se jhaank rahe the, chehre ujele, aankhen dar aur suspicion se bharpoor. Unhone raat survive kar li thi, lekin unknown ka dar ab pehle se zyada bhaari tha.

Radha ne apni awaaz buland ki, firm lekin gentle, un tak pahunchna chahti thi unke terror ke paar. “Ho gaya. Bhookepan… curse… ab dar se bandh nahi hai. Kisi ko dena nahi padega. Ab aur khoon nahi.”

Bheed mein goonj utthi. Kuch aage badhe, hesitant, jaise test kar rahe hon ki yeh baatein sach hain. Somnath chhaon se nikla, geela aur kaampa hua, brass vessel ab bhi haath mein.

“Tumne ise tod diya,” usne rukhawat se kaha. “Kuan… sun raha hai… badal gaya hai.”

“Haan,” Radha ne kaha. “Lekin sirf isliye badla kyunki maine sach bola. Gaon walon ne ise isliye feed kiya kyunki woh chosen the — nahi, kyunki woh chup rahe. Naam jo bolne nahi diye gaye, deeds jo chhupaye gaye… fear ne centuries tak iska feast kiya.”

Ek aurat aage aayi, ek bachche ko pakad kar. “Ab hum kya karen?” usne pucha. “Fasal… barish…”

Radha ne chehron ko dekha — thake hue, darte hue, guilty. Usne gehri saans li. “Hum baat karenge. Hum acknowledge karenge ki humse, hamari families se kya liya gaya. Sirf yaad rakh kar, naam le kar, accept kar kar hi hum andhere ko feed kiye bina jeene layak honge. Yeh gaon… hamari zindagiyan… hamare ancestors… sabko recognition deserve karti hain.”

Somnath ne sar jhuka liya, kaampte hue. “Aur sacrifices?”

Radha ne jhatak diya. “Ab nahi. Sacrifices nahi, sirf sach. Na dar, bas yaadein. Isi tarah balance restore hota hai.”

Gaon walay goonjne lage, awaazein chhoti magar tez hoti gayi jab woh un logon ki kahaniyan sunane lage jo le gaye gaye, chhupayi mauton, desperation mein kiye gaye bargains. Har naam jo bola gaya, zameen halki padti gayi, kichad dheere-dheere sukhta gaya, kaale nishaan fade hote gaye.

Chote bachche bhi, jo andar chhupaye gaye the, square mein nikal aaye, unki masoom awaazein apne khoon playmates ke naam lete hue. Gaon, geela aur toota hua, phir se saans lene laga.

Radha kuan ke paas gayi aur uski gehrayi mein jhanki. Pani shaant tha, subah ki pehli roshni ko reflect kar raha tha. Pehli baar, usko sukoon mehsoos hua. Kuan destroy nahi hua tha, lekin ab khoon nahi maangta tha. Woh intezaar kar raha tha — patient aur silent — ek yaadgaar ki tarah jo hua tha aur jo kabhi nahi bhoolna chahiye.

Jab gaon walay uske gird ikattha hue, Radha ne akhirkaar apne chehre par ek chhoti, thaki hui smile allow ki. Reckoning shuru ho chuka tha — na dar se, na khoon se, balki sach se.

Aur Kothivaram, geela aur nishaan se bhara hua, ab heal hone laga.

—

Chapter Fourteen: The Hunger Beyond

Radha ke gaon lautne ke baad hafton beet chuke the. Kothivaram dheere-dheere recover kar raha tha: giray hue kutiyon ko rebuild kiya gaya, kaale hue kheton ko saaf kiya gaya, aur gaon walay openly baat karne lage un cheezon ke baare mein jo generations tak chhupayi gayi thi. Kuan gaon ke center mein shaant baitha tha, ek dark sentinel jo pale morning sun ko reflect kar raha tha.

Radha ko laga ki nightmare khatam ho gaya. Lekin kabhi-kabhi, jab woh kuan ke paas se guzarti, usko ek bechaini ki thithurahat mehsoos hoti.

Ek shaam, gaon ke pond ke kinare walk karte waqt, usne kuch ajeeb notice kiya. Pani unnaturally ripple kar raha tha, hawa bilkul shaant thi. Tiny black tendrils pond ki surface se uth rahe the, ink ki tarah slow motion mein twist karte hue. Woh aur paas jhuki, spine par dar chadhne laga.

Ek awaaz phusphusayi, soft, almost imperceptible:

“Balance… broken… hunger seeks beyond…”

Radha ka pet ulta ho gaya. Usko bataya gaya tha ki kuan ka bhookepan sirf gaon se juda hai, bloodlines se, Kothivaram ke secrets aur silence se. Lekin yeh — yeh alag tha. Yahan ka pani, kuan se door, same darkness le kar chal raha tha.

Hawa badli, faint echoes of voices le kar — naam jo usne kabhi nahi suney, cries aise jagahon se jo usko identify nahi ho sakte. Bhookepan ab geography se bandh nahi tha. Fail raha tha.

Usi raat, usne purane qile ka phir se visit kiya, wapas us platform tak jahan usne lost logon ke naam liye the. Baarish ruk chuki thi, ruins silent the, lekin patthar garam mehsoos ho raha tha, subtle energy se pulse kar raha tha.

Usne apna haath symbols par press kiya, prayers murmur ki jo usne seekhi thi. Hawa moti ho gayi, multiple voices mein whispers le kar — kuch pleading, kuch mocking.

Phir awaaz aayi, clear, terrifying:

“Tumne apne gaon ko azad kar diya. Lekin duniya sab bargains ko yaad rakhti hai. Bhookepan wahan move karega jahan acknowledge nahi kiya gaya. Woh silent, hidden, forgotten ko dhundhega…”

Radha piche hati, dil tez dhadak raha tha. Uska dimaag tez chal raha tha. Usne Kothivaram ke liye curse tod diya tha, lekin countless villages, countless log jo bargains kiye ya truths chhupaye — un sab ke liye? Kuan — ya jo bhi woh ab ban chuka tha — ab sirf ek village entity nahi tha. Uska bhookepan outward shift ho chuka tha, silence ki taraf jo ise feed karti thi, kahin bhi.

Realization ka horror settle hua: yeh ant nahi tha. Kuan ka ancient bhookepan, jo kabhi confined tha, ab mobile ho chuka tha. Yeh pani ke through, rivers, ponds, streams, aur har jagah failta jayega jahan secrecy, fear, ya unspoken debts mile.

Radha ke haath muthi mein bandh gaye, nails palms mein ghus gaye. Usne ek battle jeeti thi, haan, lekin war Kothivaram ki pahadiyon se bahut aage stretch karti thi.

Jab pehla chand ruins ke upar utha, fort ke moat ki surface unnaturally ripple hui. Tiny tendrils of black water pattharon par chadh rahe the, zameen ke paar pahunchna chahte hue. Radha ne dekha, dil jam gaya.

Usne apne aap se phusphusaya, awaaz raat ke upar barely audible:

“Mujhe ise rokna hoga. Main ise failne nahi doongi. Main ise khatam karungi… har jagah.”

Door kahin, ek stream gurgled aur hissed unnaturally. Bhookepan move ho chuka tha. Aur hunt shuru ho chuki thi.

—

Chapter Fifteen: Beyond Kothivaram

Radha ne subah hone se pehle ek chhota bundle pack kiya: kuch din ka ration, apni maa ka payal, temple ka brass lamp, aur ek journal jismein usne un logon ke naam aur kahaniyan likhi thi jo kuan ne le liye the. Gaon abhi sota tha, unaware ki bhookepan unke borders se kitni door tak fail chuka hai. Woh silent houses ke paas se slip hui, pairon par kichad chipkaya hua, forest trail ki taraf chali jahan streams old fort se nikal kar rivers mein milte the.

Forest storm ke echoes se zinda tha. Daaliyan tapak rahi thi, hawa geeli mitti ki smell le kar, har kadam use unknown ki taraf le ja raha tha. Radha ka dimaag tez chal raha tha, entity ke whispers yaad kar rahi thi: hunger moves where it is unacknowledged. Woh silent, forgotten ko dhundhega. Usko pata tha ki villages jo Kothivaram se door the — places jo usne kabhi nahi dekhe — pehle hi iske influence mein ho sakte the.

Pehle signs tab dikhe jab woh river ke kinare pahunchi. Pani mota aur kaala tha, moonlight mein serpents ki tarah writhing. Radha ne ghutno tak jhuk kar haathon mein cup kiya. Familiar metallic tang uske fingers ko jala diya, aur whispers, faint magar insistent, river se uthne lage.

“Names… remember… debts unpaid…”

Usne thithuri li. Bhookepan ne seekh liya tha. Ab proximity ki zaroorat nahi thi. Woh travel kar sakta tha — pani ke through, zameen ke through — silence ko dhundh kar jahan bhi woh maujood ho. Yeh soch uska pet kharab kar deti thi.

Radha ne riverbank ke saath-saath chalna shuru kiya, flow ke saath northward. Door villages dikhe, trees ke beech se dim lights jhilmila rahi thi. Usne forest ke kinare se dekha jab log bewaqoof ban kar apni zindagi guzar rahe the, unaware ki unke streams aur wells mein creeping darkness chhupi hui hai. Har unspoken story, har secret sorrow, ise feed kar rahi thi.

Jab woh chalti rahi, usne Kothivaram ke lost logon ke naam whisper kiye, uski awaaz river ke paar carry hoti rahi. Kaala pani twitch karta, uski acknowledgment se thoda piche hat jata. Ek chhoti jeet, lekin temporary. Bhookepan vast, boundless, aur ancient tha.

Raath zyada ghanee ho gayi, aur Radha ne ek ruined watchtower mein rest kiya. Woh andar baithe, river ki sinister gurgle sunte hue. Uska journal uski god mein khula pada tha, naam jo usne bola tha pages par shield ki tarah bikhar rahe the. Usko ehsaas hua ki yeh journey endless hogi unless woh source — original shrine, pehli jagah jahan kuan bind hua tha — find kar leti. Sirf wahan hi woh hope kar sakti thi ki bhookepan ko entirely sever kiya ja sake.

Ek sudden chill uske through guzri. Pani ne phir whisper kiya, ab zyada loud, tower ke ruins mein echo karte hue:

“Tum chhup nahi sakti. Hum sab yaad rakhte hain. Aur hum sab ke liye ayenge.”

Radha ne apna dar niche nigaal liya aur muthi bandhi. Aage ka raasta use Kothivaram se bahar le jayega — unknown forests, rivers, aur villages ke ruins jo bahut pehle bhool diye gaye. Kuan ka bhookepan relentless tha, lekin uske paas woh cheez thi jo woh nahi le sakta: naam lena, yaad rakhna, aur sach ka saamna karne ki himmat.

Woh pehli roshni mein uthi, phir se muddy path par qadam rakha. River uske saath black aur mota beh raha tha, halka sa hiss karta hua, uske peeche-trailing. Journey shuru ho chuki thi — sirf Kothivaram ke liye nahi, balki har gaon ke liye jo centuries tak apne secrets chhupa raha tha.

Radha ne khud se northward chalte hue phusphusaya:

“Main ise end tak follow karungi. Main origin find karungi. Aur main ise khatam karungi… chahe jo bhi ho.”

Forest ne uski silhouette ko nigal liya, aur river uske peeche hiss karta raha. Bhookepan move kar raha tha, aur woh bhi — uske andhere ke dil ki taraf.

—

Chapter Sixteen: The First Village

Jab Radha pehle gaon ke outskirts tak pahunchi, toh shaam ho chuki thi. River jo usne follow kiya tha, ek sluggish stream mein badal gaya jo broken fences aur crumbling huts ke past snake kar raha tha. Chimneys se dhuan nikal raha tha, lekin life ki koi awaaz nahi — na hasi, na bacchon ki cheekhein, na kitchen se bartan ki khankhar.

Gaon pehli nazar mein abandoned lag raha tha. Lekin jab Radha aur paas gayi, uska dil jam gaya. Shadows huts ke beech unnaturally move kar rahe the. Windows mein flickering light thi, lekin andar koi figure nahi dikhi. Hawa moti thi, stagnant pani aur decay ki smell se bhaari.

Woh ek giray hue cart ke peeche chhipi, main square mein jhanki. Wahan, usne dekha: villagers apne kuan ke gird ikattha hue, choice se nahi, balki trance mein. Unki aankhen glassy, muh silently moving as if words repeat kar rahe hon jo unke apne nahi. Kaale pani ki streams kuan se uth rahi thi, zameen par living vines ki tarah creep kar rahi, villagers ke pairon ke gird lipat rahi.

Radha ka pet kharab ho gaya. Yeh bhookepan ka kaam tha — subtle nahi, fear ya tradition se bandh bhi nahi ab. Fail raha tha, logon ko consume kar raha tha bina ceremony ya consent ke.

Ek faint voice hawa mein break hui, eerily familiar.

“Remember… name… blood… forgotten…”

Villagers apna silent chant jaari rakhe, chehre ujele aur slack. Ek-ek karke, unke expressions twist hone lage, muscles tight hone lage jaise pani unhe apne andar kheench raha ho. Black tendrils oonchi chadhi, arms aur waists ke gird coil hui, unhe zameen se thoda utha diya. Radha unki aankhon mein dar dekh sakti thi — ya jo bhi bacha tha — before woh dissolve ho gaya submission mein.

Usne apne aap se phusphusaya, awaaz kaamp rahi thi: “Mujhe act karna hoga. Main… bas dekh kar nahi baith sakti.”

Kothivaram mein seekhe hue lessons ka istemal karke, usne villagers ke ancestors ke naam bulane shuru kiye, ek-ek karke. Un naam ka jinhe chhupaya gaya, bhoola diya gaya, silence mein dabaya gaya. Chant uske gala se utha, uneven aur desperate.

Black tendrils ne uski awaaz se thithuri li, thoda piche hata. Kuch villagers waver kiye, jaise nightmare se jaagne lage. Pani hissed, kuan ki surface violently ripple hui, lekin Radha jaari rahi, har naam jo jaanti thi, stories jo yaad thi, gaon ki hidden history ko piece karke.

Bhookepan ne cheekh mari — ek awaaz jo hawa ko hila di, daanton mein dard kar di. Phir bhi woh aage badhi. Dheere-dheere, dard se, kaala pani wapas hatne laga, tendrils kuan mein slither hui. Villagers zameen par gir gaye, haanpte hue, gehri saanson se jaise lambi, suffocating neend se jaagne lage.

Radha kuan ke paas ghenon par baith gayi, pasine aur baarish se geeli. Uski awaaz phuli hui, lungs jal rahe the, lekin usne kar diya tha. Abhi ke liye.

Lekin jab usne horizon ki taraf dekha, realization ne usko kadwa clarity se maar di: yeh sirf shuruaat thi. Bhookepan Kothivaram se bahut aage move ho chuka tha, aur har gaon jo woh pass karegi ek aur battlefield ho sakta tha.

Usne apne haathon mein journal ko zor se pakad liya.

“Main ise follow karungi,” usne raat se phusphusaya. “Main ise face karungi jahan bhi jaye. Main ise khatam karungi… chahe kitna hi fail jaye.”

Kahin upstream se, kaale pani ki ek faint ripple silently uski taraf move hui, whispers le kar jo wada karte the ki bhookepan ne use bhoola nahi.

Radha uthi, determined. Hunt sachmuch shuru ho chuki thi.

—

Chapter Seventeen: The Forest of Whispers

Radha ne stream ke saath northward follow kiya, kaala pani uske saath silent predator ki tarah move kar raha tha. Suraj neecha tha, orange aur crimson mein bleed kar raha tha, aage ke forest ko strange, almost otherworldly glow mein daal raha tha. Ped purane the — trunks twisted aur gnarled, roots path par fingers ki tarah failay hue. Patte hawa mein whisper kar rahe the, aur ek pal ke liye, Radha ko laga jaise forest khud zinda ho, dekh raha ho, intezaar kar raha ho.

Legends ne is jagah ke baare mein baat ki thi — The Forest of Whispers. Travelers ise avoid karte the, claim karte the ki yahan ruins hain villages se bhi purane, aur strange voices streams ko haunt karti hain. Uski daadi ki kahaniyan wapas aayi: “Pehle bargains yahan kiye gaye the, beti. Agar tum bhookepan ki origin dhundhna chahti ho, toh tumhe wahan jana hoga jahan yeh shuru hua.”

Har kadam aage bechaini badhata gaya. Forest unnaturally silent tha, lekin quiet ke neeche, ek low murmur river ki tarah daud raha tha. Whispers — faint, layered, impossible to localize. Naam, stories ke fragments, aur half-sentences of warnings hawa mein ghoom rahe the, uske sar ke gird curl kar rahe.

Radha ke ungliyon ne journal ko zor se pakad liya. Woh aloud bolna shuru ki — ek ritual jo usne rely karna seekh liya tha — Kothivaram ke lost logon ke naam, pehle villagers ke jo silence ne feed kiya tha. Whispers ne thithuri li, shadows ki tarah twist karke piche hate, jaise roshni se darte hue.

Achanak, forest ek clearing mein khul gaya. Beech mein ek enormous stone altar khada tha, centuries se worn, moss aur roots se dhaka hua. Uske gird broken statues aur faded symbols zameen par etched the. Yeh wahi hona chahiye — shrine jiske baare mein uski daadi ne warning di thi. Pehla bargain yahan seal hua tha.

Radha ne cautious approach kiya. Hawa invisible energy se pulse kar rahi thi, moti aur electric. Kaala pani, streams aur rivers ke saath, clearing ke kinare ruk gaya, jaise enter karne se dar raha ho. Whispers intense ho gaye, uske gird storm ki tarah ghoomne lage.

Uska dil tez dhadka jab woh altar pe chadhi. Stone par carved symbols ne uski chhoo par halki si glow ki. Ek deep, resonant awaaz pattharon se uthi, hundreds of whispers ke saath layered:

“Tum aa gayi ho. Tum origin dhundhna chahti ho. Tum khatam karna chahti ho jo shuru hua?”

Radha ne apna dar niche nigaal liya. “Haan. Main ise khatam karungi. Bhookepan ne kaafi der tak feed kiya hai. Main ise naam doongi, confront karungi, aur ise rokungi.”

Zameen hil gayi. Surrounding forest ki roots writhed, altar ke saath intertwine hui, use thoda uthaya, jaise forest khud uski resolve test kar raha ho. Whispers torrent mein badal gaye, sab ek saath bol rahe — pleas, accusations, warnings. Kuch faintly familiar the, long dead villagers ke naam. Others bilkul unknown, ancient, forgotten.

Radha ne haath uthaye, journal ko ooncha uthaya. “Main pehle bargains ko acknowledge karti hoon! Main naam leti hoon, debts, fears jo tumhe bind kiye! Main silence ko ab aur bhookepan ko feed hone nahi doongi!”

Hawa vibrate hui, ek low hum roar mein badal gaya. Forest streams ka kaala pani altar ki taraf surge hua lekin piche hata, twist karta hua, jaise uski authority se dar raha ho. Whispers clear ho gaye, structured. Voices ne uske jawab diye, unko naam diye jo bhool diye gaye. Woh aloud padhne lagi, aankhon se aansoo beh rahe the, har syllable ke saath awaaz majboot hoti gayi.

Phir usne mehsoos kiya — ek presence, older aur deeper than Kothivaram ka kuan, ek consciousness jo villages se pehle, bargains se pehle, bloodlines se pehle exist karti thi. Woh bhookepan tha, rawest, most primordial form mein, dekh raha, testing, weighing her will.

Radha seedhi hui, kaampti hui lekin adamant. “Main darri nahi hoon. Main tumhe dekhti hoon. Main tumhe naam deti hoon. Main tumhe confront karti hoon. Tum zaroori existence se zyada spread nahi karoge, aur tum silence ko ab aur feed nahi karoge.”

Zameen zor se hilayi. Kaala pani hissed, piche hata, aur ek pal ke liye, forest eerily still ho gaya. Altar bright glow karne laga, symbols ne clearing ko illuminate kiya, phir fade ho gaya. Whispers soft ho gaye, jaise uski defiance ko acknowledge kar rahe hon.

Radha ghenon par baith gayi, exhausted lekin resolute. Usne bhookepan ki origin ke saamne confront karne ka pehla kadam utha liya tha.

Lekin jab woh saans lene lagi, usko ehsaas hua ki aage kya pada hai uska enormity. Yeh forest sirf shuruaat tha — bhookepan ki reach rivers, villages, aur countless hidden bargains of centuries tak faili hui thi. Use truly end karne ke liye, use trace karna hoga very first spring tak, well ki source tak.

Aur kahin, forest ke dil mein, usko laga ki bhookepan phir se stir ho raha hai — ancient aur patient, uske agle move ka intezaar kar raha.

—

Chapter Eighteen: The First Spring

Radha ne narrow forest trail ke saath ghanton tak follow kiya, instinct se guided, sight se nahi. Dense canopy ne sirf chandni ki slivers ko zameen tak pahunchna diya, twisted roots aur gnarled trees ko silvered patterns mein daal diya. Hawa wet stone aur decaying leaves ki smell le kar, har kadam underground flowing pani ki faint hiss le kar.

Aakhirkaar, forest ek chhoti glade mein khul gaya. Beech mein ek pool of water tha — crystalline lekin unsettling. Blackened rivers se alag, yeh spring ethereal light se shimmer kar raha tha, ripples surface par move kar rahe the hawa ke bina. Radha ko turant pata chal gaya — yeh source tha. Pehla spring, jahan se sab wells, sab bhookepan emerge hua tha.

Woh cautious approach ki. Hawa energy se vibrate kar rahi thi, aur pool se ek low, resonant hum emit ho raha tha. Forest aur villages ki whispers gayab ho gayi, ek singular voice ne replace kar diya — ancient, commanding, aur utterly alien:

“Tum aa gayi ho. Tum khatam karna chahti ho jo yahan shuru hua?”

Radha ne ghoda, journal ko tight pakad liya. “Haan. Main bhookepan ko khatam karna chahti hoon. Main chahti hoon ki woh dar, silence, lies pe feed karna band kar de.”

Pani ne response mein pulse kiya, jaise uski baaton ko acknowledge kar raha ho. Shapes pool ke andar uthne lagi — faces, fully formed nahi, shifting aur dissolving like mist. Kuch familiar the: Kothivaram ke ancestors, villagers jinke naam usne liye. Others unknown, timeless beings jinki presence ne bataya ki humanity ke dominion se pehle ke centuries.

“Balance tab kho gaya jab trust ki jagah fear ne le li. Hum honor se bandhe the, phir betray kiye gaye. Aur tum — child of reckoning — tum khatam karna chahti ho jo centuries se grow hua hai.”

Radha ke ghutne kaampte the, lekin usne apni awaaz uthayi. “Main sabko acknowledge karti hoon. Main debts ka naam leti hoon. Main pehle bargains aur last ko honor karti hoon. Silence tumhe ab aur feed nahi karegi. Sach ab bola gaya hai, aur main tumhe directly confront karungi.”

Pani ne violently surge kiya, edges se splash karte hue, jaise uski himmat test kar raha ho. Depths se ek figure uthna shuru hui — ek colossal, shifting mass of water, stone, aur face-like ripples. Solid form nahi, sirf presence — ancient aur infinite. Uski awaaz Radha ke dimaag mein echo hui, time ki weight le kar.

“Bahut logon ne try kiya. Sab fail hue. Bhookepan ko khatam nahi kiya ja sakta — sirf perpetuate ya redirect kiya ja sakta hai.”

Radha ke haath kaampte the, lekin woh aur paas gayi. “Phir main ise redirect karungi. Main ise confront karungi — na dar se, na khoon se, balki remembrance se. Har naam, har story, har bargain jo ignorance ya greed mein kiya gaya — main sab bolungi.”

Usne apne journal se aloud padhna shuru kiya — villagers ke naam past aur present, forgotten ones, har settlement ke ancestors jo wells se touch hue. Uski awaaz glade mein goonji, force ke against unwavering. Spring ka pani twist hua, faces within writhing aur wailing, lekin uske words ne pakad banaye rakhi — truth aur acknowledgment ki dor.

Entity ne upward surge kiya — ek towering column of living water, body par eyes forming aur dissolving, mouths endless scream mein khulte hue. Radha ne palatkar nahi dekha. Woh naam leti rahi, stories recount karti rahi, mistakes acknowledge karti rahi, bargains ko honor karti rahi.

Phir, pehli baar, pani ruka. Voices shaant ho gayi. Column waver hua. Radha ne energy ka ek pulse feel kiya — zameen ke through, forest ke through, rivers aur streams ke through. Bhookepan mehsoos hua, acknowledge hua, aur woh piche hata — awed, constrained, bound by memory aur truth, not blood.

Radha ghenon par baith gayi, exhausted, aankhon se aansoo glade ki dew mein mil rahe the. Pehli baar, usko ehsaas hua — bhookepan contain ho gaya tha. Destroy nahi, lekin ab ek uncontrolled, ravenous force nahi tha.

Pehla spring quietly shimmer kar raha tha, uski light gentle, chand ko reflect kar rahi. Kahin door, wells ab ek naya sach carry karenge — ek silent reminder ki silence aur fear ab unhe feed nahi karenge.

Radha ne apne aap se phusphusaya, awaaz kaampti hui:

“Maine kar diya… Maine ise source pe face kiya… aur ab yeh unchecked spread nahi kar sakta.”

Lekin jab woh uthi, usko pata tha yeh ant nahi tha. Bhookepan endure karega, future generations ke liye ek test. Lekin ab, uski himmat aur sach ki wajah se, woh kabhi lies pe feed nahi karega.

Radha ne Kothivaram ki taraf wapas ka raasta liya, journal tight pakde hue. Forest still tha, spring peacefully shimmer kar rahi thi, aur mahinon mein pehli baar, usko hope mehsoos hui.

—

Chapter Nineteen: Binding the Hunger

Radha subah ke waqt Kothivaram laut aayi. Gaon ne storm ke baad khud ko slowly rebuild kiya tha, lekin black water aur giray hue banyan ka memory abhi bhi har ghar mein maujood tha. Bachche darwaazon ke peeche jhank rahe the, maaen warnings whisper kar rahi thi, aur Somnath, priest, bhi silence mein intezaar kar raha tha, unsure ki kya expect kare.

Radha ne villagers ko central square mein bulaya. Uski awaaz, though weary, authority le kar:

“Hum survive isliye kiye kyunki bhookepan ko confront kiya gaya, lekin survival kaafi nahi hai. Agar hum bhoolenge, agar hum chup rahe, toh woh wapas ayega. Hum ise bind karenge — na khoon se, na dar se, balki remembrance aur sach se.”

Villagers ne cautious suna — kuch skeptical, kuch fearful. Radha ne explain kiya ki usne pehle spring pe kya kiya, kaise usne lost logon ke naam liye, bargains ko acknowledge kiya, aur primordial bhookepan ko confront kiya. Usne river, forest, aur ruins ki baat ki, aur kaise silence ne entity ko centuries tak feed kiya.

Somnath aage aaya, finally samajh kar. “Phir humein saath mein act karna hoga. Har naam jo bola gaya, har story jo yaad ki gayi, har bargain jo acknowledge hua… isi se hum ise bind karenge.”

Radha ne sar hila diya. “Hum ek ritual banayenge. Hum apne ancestors ko, apne lost bacchon ko, aur un logon ko honor karenge jinki stories bhool di gayi. Hum woh truths bolenge jo chhupayi gayi. Sirf tab hi bhookepan contain rahega.”

Villagers ne din bhar kaam kiya. Naam cloth aur paper par likhe gaye, small shrines banaye gaye lost logon ke liye, prayers old well par whisper ki gayi. Radha ne ritual lead kiya, center mein khadi, uski awaaz villagers ke murmurs ke upar uthi. Usne naam aloud bulaye, deeds recount kiye, aur fear ya ignorance mein kiye gaye bargains ko bola.

Kuan, jo mahinon se shaant tha, faintly ripple hua, lekin black tendrils emerge nahi hue. Instead, pani liquid glass ki tarah shimmer kar raha tha, villagers ke chehron ko reflect kar raha tha. Square mein energy gently pulse kar rahi thi, almost approvingly, jaise entity khud effort ko acknowledge kar rahi ho.

Raat hone tak, villagers ne ritual complete kar liya. Woh kuan ke gird circle bana kar khade the, haath joined, voices united in remembrance aur sach. Radha ka dil bhar aaya — yeh sirf ek ritual nahi tha. Yeh ek vow tha, ek promise ki fear aur silence ab bhookepan ko feed nahi karenge.

Somnath ne sar jhukaya. “Kuan rahenga, lekin ab woh dar ya khoon nahi maangega. Woh yaad rakhega jo aaj raat yahan bola gaya.”

Radha ne halki si smile di, exhaustion limbs ko kheench rahi thi. “Aur hum bhi yaad rakheinge. Yeh gaon, aur har gaon jo bhookepan se touch hua, sach bolna seekhega, memory ko honor karega, aur kabhi nahi bhulega. Yehi tarika hai jisse woh bound rahega.”

Villagers slowly disperse hue, candles aur offerings le kar apne ghar, quiet reverence ke saath. Radha kuan ke paas rahi, pani ko moonlight mein shimmer karte dekhti rahi. Pehli baar, usko peace mehsoos hui.

Kahin zameen ke neeche, bhookepan so raha tha — contained, patient, bound by sach. Wo centuries se endure karta aaya tha, aur zyada endure karega, lekin ab woh ek bhookepan tha jo acknowledgment maangta tha, na fear.

Radha ne gehri saans li, taaron ko dekhti hue. Journey abhi khatam nahi hui — usne seekha tha ki bhookepan move kar sakta hai, silence ko feed karta hai jahan bhi milta hai. Lekin usne yeh bhi seekha ki himmat, yaadein, aur sach kisi bhi dar se zyada strong hain.

Kothivaram safe tha — abhi ke liye. Aur Radha, ladki jo well, forest, aur ancient bhookepan khud ko face kar chuki thi, jaanti thi ki usne uski story ka course forever change kar diya tha.

Hawa pedon ke through whisper kar rahi thi, gentle aur approving. Kuan softly glimmer kar raha tha, aur gaon so raha tha, finally free of its shadow.

Radha ne apne ghar ki taraf mudkar dekha, journal haath mein, rest karne ko ready lekin aware: duniya hidden bargains se bhari hui hai, aur ek din, kisi aur ko rise karna padega unhe naam dena.

Lekin aaj raat… aaj raat, Kothivaram free tha.

—

Chapter Twenty: Echoes of Remembrance
